title: How Peter McKinnon Turned $0.55 Into A YouTube Empire (Interview)
tags: marketing|creativity|youshaei|jon youshaei|creators|creator economy|jonathon youshaei|instagram employee|youtube employee|youtube|peter mckinnon|casey neistat|peter mckinnon camera bag|peter mckinnon coffee|peter mckinnon edc|peter mckinnon backpack|peter mckinnon product photography|peter mckinnon lightroom|peter mckinnon broll|peter mckinnon camera basics|peter mckinnon instagram|peter mckinnon intro|forbes|peter mckinnon forbes
description:
Peter McKinnon pursued YouTube full-time when he only had 800 subscribers and made $0.55 cents on the platform. For contrast, other creators like Graham Stephan quit his full time job when he made $1,000,000 from YouTube and Mark Rober quit his job after he had 10 million subscribers.

Today, McKinnon has over 5.5 million subscribers, films cinematic videos like The Bucket Shot, and created some of the highest quality (and best selling) merch with his Nomatic backpack, PolarPro Signature Edition II camera lens, and now his Camera Tool which he spent 18 months perfecting.

Together with Spacestation and business partners Sean Holladay and Shaun “Shonduras” McBride, he’s built a beloved brand without ever feeling like he sold out. 

In this interview, Peter and I talk about everything form his bromance with Casey Neistat, competing with the king of TikTok magic, Zach King, and how he single handedly re-invented photography content on YouTube.

Special thanks to Stir for sponsoring today's video. Stir is a financial tool for collaborating, splitting revenue, money management and metrics—all in one place. For more info: https://usestir.com/youshaei

description:
I interviewed Anthony Padilla and Ian Hecox, the co-founders of Smosh who recently bought back their historic YouTube channel after 12 years. See written piece in Forbes and studio tour below. Thank you Lickd for sponsoring: get your first 14 days of Lickd stock music for free and save on your first mainstream track here:  https://go.lickd.co/JonYoushaeiv4

Also thanks to Beacons for sponsoring: check out https://beacons.ai/i/lp/domain to claim your custom .bio domain today.

Lastly, thanks to Storyblocks for sponsoring: download unlimited stock media at one set price with Storyblocks: https://storyblocks.com/jonyoushaei

Thanks Ditto Dub for dubbing our video. To get your videos dubbed and increase you views, go here: https://dittodub.com/go

In 2011, Anthony Padilla and Ian Hecox were one of the first to sell a YouTube channel long before the likes of MrBeast started on YouTube. 12 years later, they’ve become the first to buy a channel back. We discussed Smosh’s evolution from sketch comedy to a production power house with over 35 employees, 5 channels, and 45 million combined subscribers. We also covered the duo’s 2017 split, their solo journeys as creators, and their ultimate reunion to buy back Smosh from Rhett and Link (Mythical Entertainment).

Shoutout to Ricardo Ramirez, Alessandro Bordoni, Samuel Flores and the Richy Films team for helping film this episode. Check them out here: https://richyfilms.com/

title: Tesla Cybertruck: Why Elon Musk Is A Better Marketer Than Steve Jobs (Sorry, Apple)

description:
SUBSCRIBE for new videos every week! ► https://bit.ly/SubscribeToAdmazing

The media had a field day when Elon Musk announced the Tesla Cybertruck last month. While some of them called the Cybertruck event "disastrous," I predict that we’ll remember it as one of the best product announcements since Steve Jobs unveiled the first iPhone in 2007. 

So on this episode of Admazing, we’re actually going to compare Steve Job’s iPhone announcement to Elon Musk’s Cybertruck announcement because they actually have a lot of surprising things in common. In doing so, we'll talk about the 3 things we can learn when it comes to marketing a new product.

Fair warning: if you're a fan of the Ford F150, this video may be hard to watch ; )

I interviewed Terry Crews about how he broke into Hollywood, changed YouTube with his Old Spice videos, and using deepfakes and AI to make videos after he's gone. Thanks HubSpot for sponsoring: https://clickhubspot.com/eqb

Terry Crews recently collaborated with @ZachKing on a short film called Stranded 3, which brings together the best of social and traditional media. Both King and Crews have helped redefine their respective industries over the past decade. While many know Crews as the host of America’s Got Talent and his memorable roles singing Vanessa Carlton 1,000 Miles in White Chicks and starring alongside Andy Samberg in Brooklyn Nine-Nine, he’s also a furniture designer, talented flutist, and ex-NFL player. In this interview, we went deep into Terry's career and we'll be doing the same with my upcoming interview with Zach King next. Terry also spoke about the AI company Metaphysic who is behind Deepfake Tom Cruise and developing groundbreaking new technology for the upcoming Tom Hanks movie.

title: Why YouTube Wouldn’t Exist Without The Super Bowl #shorts #superbowl
description:
Did you know YouTube may not exist as we know it without Janet Jackson’s Super Bowl wardrobe malfunction? 🤯 

YT started as a dating site (yes, actually) before pivoting to a video platform for anyone to upload clips after Janet and Justin Timberlake led millions of people to search for videos “on-demand” in 2004 vs waiting for replays on TV. 

Fast forward 17 years and it evolved into the YouTube we know today with creators like MrBeast, Dude Perfect, Jeffree Star and more making content you can enjoy anytime, anywhere.

title: Mark Rober Reveals Secret Science of Viral Videos
tags: marketing|creativity|youshaei|jon youshaei|creators|creator economy|jonathon youshaei|instagram employee|youtube employee
description:
Subscribe for more videos: https://bit.ly/YouTubeYoushaei

Mark Rober knows a thing or two about how to grow on YouTube. After all,  Mark Rober gained 10M followers while still working his day job at Nasa and Apple before growing his YouTube channel to now 20M followers. I recently sat down with Mark to ask him about his most viral videos  (including "World's Tallest Elephant Toothpaste Volcano" and "Backyard Squirrel Maze"), avoiding creator burnout, his relationship with Jimmy Kimmel and MrBeast, working at Apple and NASA and much more. We also talked about his most audacious challenge yet: working with Mr.Beast to remove 30 million pounds of trash from the ocean as part of #TeamSeas which is the sequel to 2019's #TeamTrees initiative. 

For more info and to donate to #TeamSeas, click here: https://teamseas.org

title: Spotify Reveals 3 Surprising Trends for Podcasters
tags: marketing|creativity|youshaei|jon youshaei|creators|creator economy|jonathon youshaei|instagram employee|youtube employee|youtube|podcast|podcaster
description:
Here's 3 tips for podcasters based on listening trends from Spotify's latest Fan Study 2023:

1) Podcast discovery peaks in 4 key months: January, March, July, and October. 
2) 6 pm to midnight are the peak viewing hours for video podcasts. 
3) Fans who engage with Spotify’s Q and A feature, end up listening for twice as long. 

Those were my takeaways but check out the full study here: https://pod.spoti.fi/e/FanStudyJon

Thank you to Spotify for Podcasters for your partnership on this and your support in getting our podcast, Created with Jon Youshaei, out to so many people this year.

title: How Michael Jackson Saved Pepsi With This $5 Million Super Bowl Commercial
tags: Michael Jackson|Pepsi|Super Bowl|Super Bowl Commercial|Alfonso Ribeiro|Carlton|Britney Spears|Leonardo Dicaprio|Admazing|Jon Youshaei|Youshaei
description:
SUBSCRIBE for More Admazing! ► https://bit.ly/SubscribeToAdmazing

What do Pepsi, Fresh Prince of Bel Air, and neuroscience all have in common? Two words: Michael Jackson. In 1984, Michael Jackson starred in a Pepsi commercial for a whopping $5 million. It aired during the Super Bowl and would go on to impact the way musicians, marketers, and scientists go about their jobs. 

Moreover, it would spark the careers of Alfonso Ribeiro (aka Carlton from Fresh Prince of Bel Air), Leonardo Dicaprio, Brad Pitt, Paul Rudd, and change the wayed Hollywood studios discovered actors in commercials. So on this episode of Admazing, we’re going to talk about the forgotten details of that commercial, what we can learn from it, and how it’s still impacting our lives in ways we don’t realize.
In this video, we'll break down whether this commercial Admazing, Adtrocious...or just Adequate.

title: The Mandalorian: How Disney Copied Pikachu To Create Grogu (Baby Yoda)

description:
Baby Yoda (Grogu) is Disney’s best creation since Mickey Mouse. He captured our hearts and soon he’ll capture our wallets in what most people don’t realize is a carefully calculated plan from Disney to "hack" our brain into loving Baby Yoda the same way Pokemon got us to love Pikachu. So on this episode of Admazing, we’re going to talk about the 3 lessons we can learn from the way Disney created Baby Yoda (Grogu) for the launch of The Mandalorian on Disney+

title: What Star Wars Must Learn From The Harry Potter Movies
tags: Star Wars|Harry Potter|The Rise of Skywalker|Disney|Warner Brothers|JK Rowling|George Lucas|JJ Abrams|Episode 9: The Rise of Skywalker|Admazing|Marketing|Jon Youshaei|Youshaei
description:
SUBSCRIBE for More Admazing! ► https://bit.ly/SubscribeToAdmazing

There's a reason why Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker bombed with a 52% rating on Rotten Tomatoes. And no, it's not just Disney's fault. To really understand the problem, we'll compare Disney's new Star Wars trilogy to the way Warner Brothers produced another one of the world's most beloved franchises: Harry Potter. We'll compare the popularity of the two movie franchises, analyze what went wrong, and talk about the surprising ways the studios treated George Lucas and JK Rowling.

In doing so, hopefully you’ll learn how to become a much better storyteller and marketer. No lightsabers or magic wands required : )

title: How Kobe Bryant Cold Called Oprah...Then Won An Oscar (NBA All Star Tribute)
tags: Kobe Bryant|Oscars|Academy Awards|Kobe|Gianna Bryant|Dear Basketball|Admazing|Marketing|Jon Youshaei|Youshaei
description:
SUBSCRIBE for More Admazing! ► https://bit.ly/SubscribeToAdmazing

Kobe Bryant left us too soon. I've been trying to make sense of things ever since his helicopter accident so I thought a good way to pay tribute would be to talk about why Kobe was so special. And no, I'm not just talking about how Kobe dominated with the Los Angeles Lakers on the basketball court. I'm taking about what he did off of the court; during retirement. 

Nowhere was that more obvious than during the Academy Awards when Kobe won an Oscar for his animated film "Dear Basketball." The way he went about that process and the people he called (hi, Oprah) can teach us a valuable lesson about how we can grow beyond what our peers expect of us. Rest in peace, Mamba. Thanks for all the inspiration.

title: Hamilton: How Lin-Manuel Miranda Created A Cultural Phenomenon | Disney+
tags: Hamilton|Lin-Manuel Miranda|Hamilfilm|Disney|Disney plus|Disney+|Creativity|Lin Manuel|Admazing|Marketing|Jon Youshaei|Youshaei
description:
SUBSCRIBE for More Admazing! ► https://bit.ly/SubscribeToAdmazing

Everybody is talking about Hamilton now that the musical is on Disney+ but let's talk about how Lin-Manuel Miranda created the cultural phenomenon of a broadway show. After reading Ron Chernow’s biography of Alexander Hamilton in 2009, Lin-Manuel had the idea..not to turn the book into a full-blown musical. Rather, a rap mixtape. Cool idea. But that was it. But everything changed the night President Obama invited him to perform at The White House's poetry event. 

On this episode of Admazing, we'll discuss how Lin-Manuel went against President Obama's initial request to perform a song from In The Heights, how he continued to test ideas for Hamilton, and how many other entrepreneurs ranging from Spanx founder Sara Blakely to Dropbox co-founder Drew Houston follow the same exact creative process. So what is it? What can we learn? And what the heck is a Minimal Viable Product? We'll cover all of that in this episode of Admazing.

title: Working With Zappos CEO Tony Hsieh: What He Taught Me
tags: Tony Hsieh|Zappos|RIP Tony|RIP Tony Hsieh|Zappos CEO|Forbes Tony Hsieh|Vegas|Downtown Project|What i learned|Remembering Tony Hsieh|Forbes|Admazing|Marketing|Jon Youshaei|Youshaei|tony hsieh (organization leader)|company culture|tony hsieh death|tony hsieh ted talk|tony hsieh house fire|tony hsieh interview|tony hsieh delivering happiness|tony hsieh zappos culture|zappos company culture|zappos holacracy|zappos ceo death|zappos culture|zappos shoes
description:
After the passing of Tony Hsieh, I wanted to share a memory of how I got to work with him and what I learned from him. It started in 2017, when I met the Zappos CEO at the SALT conference and mentioned my Every Vowel cartoons. Next thing I knew, he asked if I could make murals of my Every Vowel cartoons for his Zappos offices in Las Vegas, Nevada. 

When it came time to hang the murals, Tony invited me to Vegas with open arms. I stayed at the trailer park where he lived. I got to see other paintings and statues that Tony hired artists to make. And I got to feed his pet llama.

Many leaders talk about bringing joy to the workplace. Many leaders talk about taking risks on young people. About being nice to strangers. But Tony actually practiced it. All of it. He truly tried to bring more smiles and laughter to everyone around him.

Rest in peace Tony ❤️
